name: Programme Governance Setup
description:
# M1 — Programme Governance Setup

**Phase:** Phase 1 — Design & Validation (2026)
**Pillar:** Governance
**Quarter (from Table 0):** Q2 2026
**Owner (from Table 0):** CITO / PMO

## Key Deliverable (verbatim from §2 Table 0)
> Programme Charter, SAP Senior Management Committee TOR, RACI matrix, risk register

## Deliverable Content (verbatim from §3 Table 1)
### Programme Charter
**Content / What it must include:** Scope statement; objectives; 3 pillars definition; success criteria; governance structure; budget envelope; link to UNESCORE 3.0
**Producer:** CITO / PMO
**Approver:** CITO + Sponsors

### RACI Matrix
**Content / What it must include:** All roles: Steering Committee, CITO/PMO, DBS SAP team, DBS EPM team, FIN, DBM, BSP, Programme Officers
**Producer:** PMO
**Approver:** Steering Cmt

### Risk & Issue Register
**Content / What it must include:** Risks per pillar (FM/PS/GM); probability x impact; owner; mitigation action;
**Producer:** PMO + Workstream Leads
**Approver:** Steering Cmt

### Steering Committee TOR
**Content / What it must include:** Meeting frequency (monthly recommended); escalation path; decision rights; reporting format
**Producer:** CITO / PMO
**Approver:** Steering Cmt

## Related actions (verbatim from §5 Phased Action Plan)
- *Technical Team Actions (DBS SAP + DBS EPM)* — Set up dedicated sandbox environment for redesign testing — isolated from 2026 operational work (Q2 2026)
- *Business Team Actions (FIN, DBM, BSP)* — Provide named business owners and deputies for each pillar by May 2026 — no analysis can start without a confirmed business counterpart (Q2 2026)

---
*Source: SAP Core Redesign Programme Framework, Version 1.0, April 2026. All text above is verbatim from the source document — no paraphrasing.*
key_deliverable_verbatim: Programme Charter, SAP Senior Management Committee TOR, RACI matrix, risk register

deliverable_details.content: Scope statement; objectives; 3 pillars definition; success criteria; governance structure; budget envelope; link to UNESCORE 3.0

deliverable_details.name: RACI Matrix

name: FM Impact Analysis Completed
description:
# M2 — FM Impact Analysis Completed

**Phase:** Phase 1 — Design & Validation (2026)
**Pillar:** FM
**Quarter (from Table 0):** Q3 2026
**Owner (from Table 0):** DBS (SAP) + FIN + DBM

## Key Deliverable (verbatim from §2 Table 0)
> FM Gap Analysis Report: as-is vs to-be for RP, MCA, OPF, VC funds

## Deliverable Content (verbatim from §3 Table 1)
### FM Gap Analysis Report & Design
**Content / What it must include:** As-is FM configuration per fund (RP, MCA, OPF, VC); one-to-one coupling issues documented; to-be model with decoupled fund/project for all sources of funds; impact on budget controls; Core Manager dependencies
**Producer:** DBS (SAP) + FIN + DBM
**Approver:** Steering Cmt

## Related actions (verbatim from §5 Phased Action Plan)
- *Technical Team Actions (DBS SAP + DBS EPM)* — Document as-is FM configuration per fund source: fund types, budget profiles, availability controls, posting keys, RP/MCA/OPF/VC coverage gaps (Q2 2026)
- *Technical Team Actions (DBS SAP + DBS EPM)* — Perform FM impact analysis: model decoupling of project-budget tight coupling; identify transaction-level impacts on active projects (Q3 2026)
- *Business Team Actions (FIN, DBM, BSP)* — Co-author FM to-be requirements with DBS SAP: define expected fund lifecycle rules, budget control expectations per fund source, reporting needs (Q3 2026)

---
*Source: SAP Core Redesign Programme Framework, Version 1.0, April 2026. All text above is verbatim from the source document — no paraphrasing.*
key_deliverable_verbatim: FM Gap Analysis Report: as-is vs to-be for RP, MCA, OPF, VC funds

deliverable_details.name: FM Gap Analysis Report & Design
deliverable_details.content: As-is FM configuration per fund (RP, MCA, OPF, VC); one-to-one coupling issues documented; to-be model with decoupled fund/project for all sources of funds; impact on budget controls; Core Manager dependencies

actions.team: Technical Team Actions (DBS SAP + DBS EPM)

actions.team: Technical Team Actions (DBS SAP + DBS EPM)

Co-author FM to-be requirements with DBS SAP: define expected fund lifecycle rules, budget control expectations per fund source, reporting needs (Q3 2026)

name: FM Sandbox Data Mapping
description:
# M3 — FM Sandbox Data Mapping

**Phase:** Phase 1 — Design & Validation (2026)
**Pillar:** FM
**Quarter (from Table 0):** Q4 2026
**Owner (from Table 0):** DBS (SAP) + FIN + DBM

## Key Deliverable (verbatim from §2 Table 0)
> Sandbox environment configured; fund structures mapped for all 4 sources. Signed-off by business.

## Deliverable Content (verbatim from §3 Table 1)
### FM Sandbox Mapping Document
**Content / What it must include:** Sample fund structures for each source in sandbox; before/after screenshots; mapping rules; data quality issues flagged; Core Manager alignment
**Producer:** DBS (SAP)
**Approver:** DBM + FIN

## Related actions (verbatim from §5 Phased Action Plan)
- *Technical Team Actions (DBS SAP + DBS EPM)* — Produce FM sandbox data mapping: configure sample fund structures for all 4 sources with actual data; document mapping rules and data quality gaps (Q4 2026)
- *Business Team Actions (FIN, DBM, BSP)* — Validate FM sandbox mappings: confirm that proposed fund structures reflect actual/adjusted budget management practices; identify edge cases (Q4 2026)

---
*Source: SAP Core Redesign Programme Framework, Version 1.0, April 2026. All text above is verbatim from the source document — no paraphrasing.*

deliverable_details.content: Sample fund structures for each source in sandbox; before/after screenshots; mapping rules; data quality issues flagged; Core Manager alignment

actions.text: Produce FM sandbox data mapping: configure sample fund structures for all 4 sources with actual data; document mapping rules and data quality gaps (Q4 2026)
Validate FM sandbox mappings: confirm that proposed fund structures reflect actual/adjusted budget management practices; identify edge cases (Q4 2026)

name: PS/WBS Impact Analysis Completed
description:
# M4 — PS/WBS Impact Analysis Completed

**Phase:** Phase 1 — Design & Validation (2026)
**Pillar:** PS-WBS
**Quarter (from Table 0):** Q3 2026
**Owner (from Table 0):** DBS (SAP) + FIN + DBM + BSP

## Key Deliverable (verbatim from §2 Table 0)
> WBS Re-modelling Report: level 4 structure defined, decoupling validated

## Deliverable Content (verbatim from §3 Table 1)
### WBS Re-Modelling Report & Design
**Content / What it must include:** Current WBS inconsistencies documented; proposed Level 4 structure (thematic/geographic + activity level); to-be model and process; SAP control rules; alignment with Core Manager sync logic
**Producer:** DBS (SAP) + FIN + DBM
**Approver:** Steering Cmt

## Related actions (verbatim from §5 Phased Action Plan)
- *Technical Team Actions (DBS SAP + DBS EPM)* — Document as-is WBS inconsistencies: field multi-usage, discrepancies, Core Manager sync, missing Level 4 structure (Q2 2026)
- *Technical Team Actions (DBS SAP + DBS EPM)* — Perform PS/WBS impact analysis: model Level 4 WBS structure; for all sources of funds; identify SAP control requirements (Q2 2026)
- *Business Team Actions (FIN, DBM, BSP)* — Co-author PS/WBS to-be requirements: define Level 4 WBS naming conventions, activity types, Output mapping rules, project specific needs (Q3 2026)

---
*Source: SAP Core Redesign Programme Framework, Version 1.0, April 2026. All text above is verbatim from the source document — no paraphrasing.*
key_deliverable_verbatim: WBS Re-modelling Report: level 4 structure defined, decoupling validated

deliverable_details.name: WBS Re-Modelling Report & Design
deliverable_details.content: Current WBS inconsistencies documented; proposed Level 4 structure (thematic/geographic + activity level); to-be model and process; SAP control rules; alignment with Core Manager sync logic

Co-author PS/WBS to-be requirements: define Level 4 WBS naming conventions, activity types, Output mapping rules, project specific needs (Q3 2026)

name: Business Partners Impact Analysis (Donor)

## Key Deliverable (verbatim from §2 Table 0)
> BP Typology Design Document: donors, customers, vendors, members

## Deliverable Content (verbatim from §3 Table 1)
### Business Partner Typology Document
**Content / What it must include:** Typology definitions: donor, customer, vendor, member state, meeting participant; migration rules from SAP customer to BP; classification logic; volume estimate (15,000 vendors/year)
**Producer:** DBS (SAP) + FIN + DBM + BSP
**Approver:** Steering Cmt

- *Business Team Actions (FIN, DBM, BSP)* — Provide vendor/customer/donor master data typology breakdown to DBS for BP analysis (Q3 2026)

---

name: Core Manager Integration Validated

**Approver:** DBM + FIN + BSP

## Related actions (verbatim from §5 Phased Action Plan)

name: User Acceptance Testing (UAT) - FM & PS
summary: M11 · User Acceptance Testing (UAT) - FM & PS
description:
# M11 — User Acceptance Testing (UAT) - FM & PS

**Phase:** Phase 2 — Implementation & Deployment (2027)
**Pillar:** FM, PS-WBS
**Quarter (from Table 0):** Q3 2027
**Owner (from Table 0):** FIN + DBM + DBS

## Key Deliverable (verbatim from §2 Table 0)
> UAT sign-off by Finance & Programme business owners

## Deliverable Content (verbatim from §3 Table 1)
### UAT Plan & Sign-off Document
**Content / What it must include:** Test scenarios by business process (carry forward, BOR, fund allocation, project creation, expense reporting); UAT participants; pass/fail criteria; signed acceptance
**Producer:** FIN + DBM + BSP
**Approver:** Steering Committee

## Related actions (verbatim from §5 Phased Action Plan)
- *Technical Team Actions* — Support UAT: provide test environment, assist with data setup, log and fix defects raised by business testers (Q3 2027)
- *Business Team Actions* — Lead UAT: design business test scenarios covering carry-forward, BOR processing, fund allocation, project creation, expense reporting; execute tests; log and accept/reject results (Q3 2027)
- *Business Team Actions* — Provide formal UAT sign-off by named business sponsor before go-live approval (Q3 2027)

---
*Source: SAP Core Redesign Programme Framework, Version 1.0, April 2026. All text above is verbatim from the source document — no paraphrasing.*

name: FM & PS Production Deployment
description:
# M12 — FM & PS Production Deployment

**Phase:** Phase 2 — Implementation & Deployment (2027)
**Pillar:** FM, PS-WBS
**Quarter (from Table 0):** Q4 2027
**Owner (from Table 0):** DBS (SAP) + CITO/PMO

## Key Deliverable (verbatim from §2 Table 0)
> Go-live: FM re-normalised, PS Level 4 WBS live; cutover plan executed

## Deliverable Content (verbatim from §3 Table 1)
### Cutover & Migration Plan
**Content / What it must include:** Data migration approach; cutover sequence; rollback plan; communication & memos; go-live checklist; hypercare period definition
**Producer:** DBS
DBM + FIN + BSP (memos)
**Approver:** Steering Cmt

### Training Materials
**Content / What it must include:** Role-based guides: Finance & Budget users, Programme Officers; updated SOPs
**Producer:** DBS
**Approver:** DBM + FIN + BSP

## Related actions (verbatim from §5 Phased Action Plan)
- *Technical Team Actions* — Develop and execute cutover plan: data migration approach, go-live sequence, rollback procedure, cutover communication to all institutes (Q3-Q4 2027)
- *Technical Team Actions* — Execute production deployment; provide hypercare support for 12 weeks post go-live (Q4 2027)
- *Business Team Actions* — Prepare memos, participate in communication and change management: inform programme officers of new WBS rules; provide feedback on training materials (Q3 2027)
- *Business Team Actions* — Train finance and programme teams using DBS-produced training materials; ensure all users complete training before go-live (Q3-Q4 2027)

---
*Source: SAP Core Redesign Programme Framework, Version 1.0, April 2026. All text above is verbatim from the source document — no paraphrasing.*
